stimulus: The fortified town of Carcassonne, which has been continuously inhabited since the Roman era, continues to draw the attention of medievalists. Unfortunately, as ______blank have observed, nineteenth-century restorations introduced ornamental features that obscure earlier defensive design, causing valuable evidence about military architecture to be misread.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The common-noun role "historians" is indefinite (no "the" or prior unique reference), so the coordinated proper-noun pair "Marc Bouchard and Camille Veron" functions restrictively as essential identification. No punctuation should separate the role from the names.

Choice A is incorrect because setting off the names with paired commas illogically implies that historians in general bear those specific names. Choice B is incorrect because the opening comma similarly disrupts the restrictive role-and-name pair. Choice C is incorrect because no comma is needed within the coordinated noun phrase "Marc Bouchard and Camille Veron."
